intro: Gesneria cinnabarina, also known as the Scarlet Gesneria or Cinnabar Scarlet, is a species of flowering plant in the Gesneriaceae family, native to Mexico and Central America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Gesneria cinnabarina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used to make topical creams and ointments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
